search_queries_used: site:kdigo.org 2024 CKD albuminuria ACR PCR confirmation hematuria
site:kdigo.org 2024 CKD referral significant albuminuria hematuria eGFR decline
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Disease-specific treatment does not cover the broad documentation workflow.
Medicine protocols and autonomous risk calculations were prohibited.
population_applicability: Partial: KDIGO includes adult, child, CKD, cause, albuminuria, haematuria, and referral qualifiers not encoded in the broad proteinuria workflow.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for selected measurement, trend, interpretation-factor, renal-function, and referral documentation, not diagnosis or treatment.
UAE_applicability: No exact current UAE proteinuria guideline was identified. KDIGO thresholds and referral implementation require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The current official KDIGO 2024 guideline and status page were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: KDIGO 2024 supersedes the 2012 CKD guideline; no newer albuminuria chapter was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped proteinuria identity and repeated or prior-result context without diagnosing CKD.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped medication-history, urinalysis, and renal-function context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped ACR, proteinuria, urinalysis, haematuria interpretation, and confirmatory-measurement fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports ACR, PCR, urinalysis, quantitative confirmation, and interpretation factors including haematuria, menstruation, exercise, and infection.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped ACR trend and clinician-arranged reassessment after a meaningful increase.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped albuminuria-with-haematuria, renal-decline, and clinician-decided referral only with Figure 48 qualifiers.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

unresolved_source_gaps: Oedema, nephrotic-range terminology, nephrotic-syndrome features, diabetes or hypertension context, general vitals, weight, lifestyle advice, medication plan, safety-netting, fixed intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow does not encode age, CKD status, transient causes, confirmatory sample, albuminuria category, haematuria threshold, or referral qualifiers.
Diagnosis, investigation selection, treatment, medicine changes, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
